[ToolUseBlock(id='toolu_01URUHNsZXpgj5YrW155rSFX', name='Write', input={'file_path': '/home/cpatrick/kryptos/scripts/e_unscramble_01_grille_permutations.py', 'content': '#!/usr/bin/env python3\n"""\nE-UNSCRAMBLE-01: Comprehensive grille-based unscrambling permutation search.\n\nParadigm: carved K4 = SCRAMBLE(real_CT). Grille defines the unscrambling permutation.\nFind permutation σ such that σ(carved_K4) = real_CT, then decrypt real_CT.\n\nApproaches:\n  1.  Rank-perm from extract[0:97] in KA order\n  2.  Rank-perm from extract[0:97] in AZ order\n  3.  KA-position mod 97 → permutation\n  4.  Extract-keyed columnar transpositions (W=5..24)\n  5.  Physical reading orders (boustrophedon, column-major, diagonal)\n  6.  Spiral reading orders\n  7.  Grille overlay: K4 in W-wide grid, holes → reading order\n  8.  T-position columnar transposition\n  9.  Direct test: first 97 of extract AS real CT\n 10.  Extract[0:97] letters pair with CT → positional map\n 11.  Hole-position flat indices mod 97 → permutation\n 12.  Reverse/mirror/interleave K4 variants\n 13.  Extract sub-selections (every 2nd, skip first N)\n 14.  Ranked hole column indices → transposition\n 15.  Extract differential: (extract_pos[i] - ct_pos[i]) mod 26 → positions\n"""\n\nimport sys, json, math, os, itertools\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nsys.path.insert(0, \'src\')\nfrom kryptos.kernel.constants import CT\n\nassert len(CT) == 97, f"Expected 97, got {len(CT)}"\n\nGRILLE_EXTRACT = \'HJLVACINXZHUYOCMWSEAFYBZACJFHIFXRYVFIJMXEILLNELJNXZKILKRDINPADMNVZACEIMUWAFGIMUKRGILVHNQXWYABXZKIKJUFQRXCD\'\nassert len(GRILLE_EXTRACT) == 106\n\nKA  = \'KRYPTOSABCDEFGHIJLMNQUVWXZ\'\nAZ  = \'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ\'\nassert len(KA) == 26\n\nCRIB1 = \'EASTNORTHEAST\'   # length 13\nCRIB2 = \'BERLINCLOCK\'     # length 11\nKEYWORDS = [\'KRYPTOS\', \'PALIMPSEST\', \'ABSCISSA\', \'SHADOW\', \'BERLIN\', \'CLOCK\',\n            \'NORTH\', \'EAST\', \'LAYER\', \'ILLUSION\', \'IQLUSION\']\n\n# ── Quadgram scorer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n_QUAD, _FLOOR = None, None\ndef load_quads():\n    global _QUAD, _FLOOR\n    if _QUAD is not None:\n        return\n    data = json.load(open(\'data/english_quadgrams.json\'))\n    # Values are already log10 probabilities\n    _QUAD  = data\n    _FLOOR = min(data.values()) - 1.0\n\ndef quad_score(text):\n    load_quads()\n    s = 0.0\n    for i in range(len(text) - 3):\n        s += _QUAD.get(text[i:i+4], _FLOOR)\n    return s / max(len(text) - 3, 1)\n\n# ── Cipher engines ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _prep_key(key, alpha):\n    return [alpha.index(k) for k in key if k in alpha]\n\ndef vigenere_dec(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = _prep_key(key, alpha); n = len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out = []\n    ki = 0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha:\n            out.append(alpha[(alpha.index(c) - kv[ki % len(kv)]) % n]); ki += 1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef beaufort_dec(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = _prep_key(key, alpha); n = len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out = []\n    ki = 0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha:\n            out.append(alpha[(kv[ki % len(kv)] - alpha.index(c)) % n]); ki += 1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef variant_beau_dec(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    """Variant Beaufort: pt = ct XOR key (same as Vigenère encrypt with key)"""\n    kv = _prep_key(key, alpha); n = len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out = []\n    ki = 0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha:\n            out.append(alpha[(alpha.index(c) + kv[ki % len(kv)]) % n]); ki += 1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\n# ── Permutation helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef apply_perm(seq, perm):\n    """Read seq in the order given by perm: result[i] = seq[perm[i]]"""\n    return \'\'.join(seq[perm[i]] for i in range(len(perm)))\n\ndef invert_perm(perm):\n    inv = [0] * len(perm)\n    for i, v in enumerate(perm): inv[v] = i\n    return inv\n\ndef rank_perm(values):\n    """Return indices sorted by values (stable). Gives permutation of 0..n-1."""\n    return sorted(range(len(values)), key=lambda i: values[i])\n\n# ── Crib & decryption sweep ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\nall_results = []\n\ndef try_decrypt(label, candidate_ct, perm=None):\n    """Try all keyword × cipher × alphabet combos on candidate_ct.\n    Returns best (score, keyword, cipher, alpha, pt) and records CRIB HITS."""\n    best = (-999.0, None, None, None, \'\')\n    for kw in KEYWORDS:\n        for alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n            for fname, fn in [(\'vig\', vigenere_dec), (\'beau\', beaufort_dec), (\'vbeau\', variant_beau_dec)]:\n                pt = fn(candidate_ct, kw, alpha)\n                if not pt: continue\n                sc = quad_score(pt)\n                has1 = CRIB1 in pt\n                has2 = CRIB2 in pt\n                entry = {\n                    \'label\': label,\n                    \'keyword\': kw,\n                    \'cipher\': fname,\n                    \'alpha\': alpha_name,\n                    \'ct\': candidate_ct,\n                    \'pt\': pt,\n                    \'score\': round(sc, 5),\n                    \'has_crib1\': has1,\n                    \'has_crib2\': has2,\n                    \'CRIB_HIT\': has1 or has2,\n                }\n                if has1 or has2:\n                    all_results.append(entry)\n                    print(f"  *** CRIB HIT *** {label} | {kw}/{fname}/{alpha_name}")\n                    print(f"      PT: {pt}")\n                if sc > best[0]:\n                    best = (sc, kw, fname, alpha_name, pt)\n    return best\n\ndef register(label, candidate_ct, perm=None):\n    """Register a candidate unscrambled CT: try decryption, track top score."""\n    sc, kw, fname, aname, pt = try_decrypt(label, candidate_ct, perm)\n    top_scores.append((sc, label, candidate_ct, kw, fname, aname, pt))\n\ntop_scores = []\n\n# ── Binary mask on-grid cells with value 0\nfor r, row_str in enumerate(MASK_RAW):\n    for c, ch in enumerate(row_str):\n        if ch == \'~\': continue\n        mask_cells.append((r, c, int(ch)))\n        if ch == \'0\':\n            holes.append((r, c))\n\nprint(f"Mask: {len(mask_cells)} on-grid cells, {len(holes)} holes (0s)")\nprint(f"K4 CT ({len(CT)}): {CT}")\nprint(f"Extract ({len(GRILLE_EXTRACT)}): {GRILLE_EXTRACT[:50]}...")\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH 1 & 2: Rank-permutation from first 97 extract chars\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH 1-2: Rank perm from extract[0:97] ──")\ne97 = GRILLE_EXTRACT[:97]\n\nfor alpha_name, alpha in [(\'KA\', KA), (\'AZ\', AZ)]:\n    ka_vals = [alpha.index(c) if c in alpha else 99 for c in e97]\n    perm = rank_perm(ka_vals)   # perm[i] = which extract position has i-th smallest value\n    # result[i] = CT[perm[i]]  →  read CT in the order given by perm\n    unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n    register(f\'rank_extract97_{alpha_name}_fwd\', unsc, perm)\n\n    inv = invert_perm(perm)\n    unsc_inv = apply_perm(CT, inv)\n    register(f\'rank_extract97_{alpha_name}_inv\', unsc_inv, inv)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH 3: KA-position mod 97 → permutation (first-occurrence)\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH 3: KA-pos mod 97 first-occurrence ──")\nfor Boustrophedon column-major (alternating column direction)\n    boustro_cm = []\n    for c in range(W):\n        col_indices = [r*W+c for r in range(n_rows) if r*W+c < 97]\n        if c % 2 == 1: col_indices = col_indices[::-1]\n        boustro_cm.extend(col_indices)\n    if len(boustro_cm) == 97 and len(set(boustro_cm)) == 97:\n        register(f\'boustro_cm_W{W}\', apply_perm(CT, boustro_cm), boustro_cm); register(f\'subset_{sname}_rank_{alpha_name}\', unsc, perm)\n        inv = invert_perm(perm)\n        register(f\'subset_{sname}_rank_{alpha_name}_inv\', apply_perm(CT, inv), inv)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH 14: Ranked hole-column indices as transposition key ──")\nhole_cols = [c for (r, c) in holes]  # column index of each hole, in reading order\n# For each width W, take first W hole-column values as the key\nfor W in range(5, 25):\n    key_vals = hole_cols[:W]\n    col_order = sorted(range(W), key=lambda i: key_vals[i])\n    n_rows = math.ceil(97 / W)\n    perm = []\n    for c in col_order:\n        for r in range(n_rows):\n            idx = r * W + c\n            if idx < 97: ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH 15: Extract-CT differential\n# diff[i] = (extract_pos[i] - ct_pos[i]) mod 26  → mapped to 0..96\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH 15: Extract-CT differential permutation ──")\nfor alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', alpha else 0\n        diffs.append((e_pos - c_pos) % n)\n    # Use diffs as ranking key\n    perm = rank_perm(diffs)\n    unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n    register(f\'diff_perm_{alpha_name}_rank\', unsc, perm)\n    inv = invert_perm(perm)\n    register(f\'diff_perm_{alpha_name}_rank_inv\', apply_perm(CT, inv), inv)\n    # Also: use diff values directly as indices mod 97\n    mod_vals = [d % 97 unsc2 = apply_perm(CT, perm2)\n        register(f\'diff_mod97_{alpha_name}_fwd\', unsc2, perm2)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH 16: has1 or has2,\n        }\n        if has1 or has2:\n            all_results.append(entry)\n            print(f"  *** CRIB HIT via extract direct key *** {entry[\'label\']}")\n        top_scores.append((sc, entry[\'label\'], CT, \'EXTRACT\', fname, alpha_name, pt))\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH 17: Grille hole ROWS as APPROACH 17: Hole-row grouped interleaving ──")\n# Group holes by row: hole_rows[r] = list of (r,c) pairs in that row\nhole_rows = defaultdict(list)\nfor r, c in holes:\n    hole_rows[r].append((r, c))\n\n# For each width W, map hole (r,c) → K4 position\nfor W in [9, 10, 11, 12, 13]:\n    n_rows_k4 = math.ceil(97 / W)\n    # Assign each hole to a K4 position if (r < n_rows_k4) and (c < W) and r*W+c < 97\n    groups = defaultdict(list)\n    for r, c in holes:\n        k4_pos = r * W + c\n        if k4_pos < 97:\n            groups[r].append(k4_pos)\n    # Read groups in row-order (by row index ascending)\n    perm = []\n    seen = set()\n    for row_idx in sorted(groups.keys()):\n        for pos in sorted(groups[row_idx]):  # within row, left-to-right\n            if pos not in seen: seen.add(pos); perm.append(pos)\n    # If we got < 97, add the missing ones at the end\n    missing = [p for p in range(97) if p not in seen]\n    perm.extend(missing)\n    if len(perm) == 97 and len(set(perm)) == 97:\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n        register(f\'hole_row_groups_W{W}\', unsc, perm)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH 18: Grille extract as substitution alphabet ──")\n# Build mapping: AZ letter i → first occurrence of KA[i] in extract\n# Use this as a cipher key\nunique_extract = []\nseen = set()\nfor c in GRILLE_EXTRACT:\n    if c not in seen: seen.add(c); unique_extract.append(c)\n\n# Map AZ positions to extract-alphabet positions\n# For each CT char, find its position in the extract\'s alphabet and convert\nfor alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n    # Build a 26-char key: for each perm\n\n# Try reversing each "row" for various widths\nfor W in [9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 17, 24, 33]:\n    n_rows = math.ceil(97 / W)\n    # Reverse every other row\n    segs = [(r*W, min((r+1)*W, 97)) c) in HOLES:\n    if r < len(TABLEAU_ROWS):\n        row_str = TABLEAU_ROWS[r]\n        if c < len(row_str) and row_str[c] not in (\' \', \'~\'):\n            extracted.append(row_str[c])\nprint(f"Extracted from tableau at holes: {\'\'.join(extracted[:50])}... (len={len(extracted)})")\nprint(f"Known extract (first 50): {GRILLE_EXTRACT[:50]}")\n\n# ── Quadgrams ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n_QUAD = json.load(open(\'data/english_quadgrams.json\'))\n_FLOOR = min(_QUAD.values()) - 1.0\ndef quad_score(text):\n    s = sum(_QUAD.get(text[i:i+4], _FLOOR) for i in range(len(text)-3))\n    return s / max(len(text)-3, 1)\n\n# ── Cipher engines ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef _kv(key, alpha):\n    return [alpha.index(k) for k in key if k in alpha]\n\ndef vig(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = _kv(key, alpha); n = len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out=[]; ki=0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha: out.append(alpha[(alpha.index(c)-kv[ki%len(kv)])%n]); ki+=1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef beau(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = _kv(key, alpha); n = len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out=[]; ki=0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha: out.append(alpha[(kv[ki%len(kv)]-alpha.index(c))%n]); ki+=1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef vbeau(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = _kv(key, alpha); n = len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out=[]; ki=0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha: out.append(alpha[(alpha.index(c)+kv[ki%len(kv)])%n]); ki+=1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef apply_perm(seq, perm):\n    return \'\'.join(seq[perm[i]] for i in range(len(perm)))\n\ndef invert_perm(perm):\n    inv=[0]*len(perm)\n    for i,v in enumerate(perm): inv[v]=i\n    return inv\n\ndef rank_perm(vals):\n    return sorted(range(len(vals)), key=lambda i: vals[i])\n\n# ── Scoring & result tracking ──────────────────────────────────────────────\nall_crib_hits = []\ntop_scores = []\n\ndef sweep(label, candidate_ct, perm=None):\n    """Try all keyword × cipher × alphabet combos."""\n    best = (-999.0, None, None, None, \'\')\n    for kw in KEYWORDS:\n        for aname, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n            for fname, fn in [(\'vig\', vig), (\'beau\', beau), (\'vbeau\', vbeau)]:\n                pt = fn(candidate_ct, kw, alpha)\n                if not pt: continue\n                sc = quad_score(pt)\n                has1 = CRIB1 in pt; has2 = CRIB2 in pt\n label, candidate_ct,\n                       best[1], best[2], best[3], best[4]))\n    return best\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH A: ──")\nfound_A = 0\nfor W in range(5, 40):\n    # Map each hole to a K4 position\n    selected = []\n    for (r, c) in HOLES:\n        k4_pos = r * W + c\n        if k4_pos < 97:\n            selected.append(k4_pos)\n    n_sel = len(selected)\n    n_unique = len(set(selected))\n    if n_unique == 97 and n_sel == 97:\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, selected)\n        sweep(f\'grille_overlay_W{W}\', unsc, selected)\n        found_A += 1\n        print(f"  W={W}: valid permutation! {n_sel} positions selected")\n    elif n_unique == 97 and n_sel > 97:\n        # More than 97 selected but all unique — doesn\'t happen if grid is contiguous\n        pass\n    elif 90 <= n_sel <= 107 and n_unique >= 90:\n        print(f"  W={W}: {n_sel} selected, {n_unique} unique (partial coverage)")\nprint(f"  Found {found_A} valid grille overlays")\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH B: 107-hole sequence → 97 K4 positions\n# Treat holes as a sequence of 107 values; select 97 using various rules\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH B: 107-hole sequence → 97 K4 positions ──")\n\n# B1: Hole flat indices mod 97 (first-occurrence)\nfor row_width in [28, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33]:\n    vals = [(r * row_width + c) % 97 for (r, c) in HOLES]\n    seen = set(); perm = []\n    for v in vals:\n        if v not in seen: seen.add(v); perm.append(v)\n        if len(perm) == 97: break\n    if len(perm) == 97:\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n        sweep(f\'hole_flatmod97_rw{row_width}_fwd\', unsc, perm)\n        inv = invert_perm(perm)\n        sweep(f\'hole_flatmod97_rw{row_width}_inv\', apply_perm(CT, inv), inv)\n\n# B2: Hole row * 28 + col_within_alphabet (restrict to cols 1-26 = alpha cols)\n# Tableau col 0 = row label, cols 1-26 = KA alphabet\nalpha_holes = [(r, c-1) for (r, c) in HOLES if 1 <= c <= 26]\nprint(f"  Holes in alphabet cols (1-26): {len(alpha_holes)}")\nif len(alpha_holes) >= 97:\n    # Use first 97\n    perm_alpha = [(r * 26 + c) % 97 for (r, c) in alpha_holes[:97]]\n    seen = set(); perm_a = []\n    for v in perm_alpha:\n        if v not in seen: seen.add(v); perm_a.append(v)\n        if len(perm_a) == 97: break\n    if len(perm_a) == 97:\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm_a)\n        sweep(\'hole_alpha_cols_mod97\', unsc, perm_a)\n\n# B3: Holes sorted by column-major order (read columns left-to-right)\nholes_colmaj = sorted(HOLES, key=lambda x: (x[1], x[0]))\nholes_rowmaj = HOLES  # already row-major\nfor name, ordered_holes in [(\'col_major\', holes_colmaj), (\'row_major\', holes_rowmaj)]:\n    for W in [26, 28, 33]:\n        vals = [(r * W + c) % 97 for (r, c) in ordered_holes]\n        seen = set(); perm = []\n        for v in vals:\n            if v not in seen: seen.add(v); perm.append(v)\n            if len(perm) == 97: break\n        if len(perm) == 97:\n (r, c) in HOLES]  # 107 column indices\n\nfor W in range(5, 30):\n    key_vals = hole_cols[:W]\n    col_order = sorted(range(W), key=lambda i: key_vals[i])\n    n_rows = math.ceil(97 / W)\n    perm = []\n    for c in col_order:\n        for r in range(n_rows):\n            idx = r * W + c\n            if idx < 97: perm.append(idx)\n    if len(perm) == 97 and len(set(perm)) == 97:\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n        sweep(f\'holecol_trans_W{W}\', unsc, perm)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH D: Hole letter → KA position → K4 index\n# Each hole at (r,c) has a tableau letter. → K4 position ──")\nhole_letters = []\nfor (r, c) in HOLES:\n    if r < len(TABLEAU_ROWS):\n        row_str = TABLEAU_ROWS[r]\n        if c < len(row_str) and row_str[c].isalpha():\n            hole_letters.append(row_str[c])\n        else:\n            hole_letters.append(\'?\')\n    else:\n        hole_letters.append(\'?\')\n# Remove \'?\'\nvalid_hole_letters = [(i, l) for i, l in enumerate(hole_letters) if l != \'?\']\nprint(f"  Valid hole letters: {len(valid_hole_letters)}")\nprint(f"  First 20: {\'\'.join(l for _,l in valid_hole_letters[:20])}")\nprint(f"  Matches extract: {\'\'.join(l for _,l in valid_hole_letters[:20])} == {GRILLE_EXTRACT[:20]}? {all(l==GRILLE_EXTRACT[i] for i,l in enumerate([l for _,l in valid_hole_letters[:20]]))}")\n\n# Use KA positions of hole letters as permutation keys\nall_hole_letters_str = \'\'.join(l for _, l in valid_hole_letters)\nif len(all_hole_letters_str) >= 97:\n    e97 = all_hole_letters_str[:97]\n    for alpha_name, alpha in [(\'KA\', KA), (\'AZ\', AZ)]:\n        vals = [alpha.index(c) if c in alpha else 99 for c in e97]\n        perm = rank_perm(vals)\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n        sweep(f\'hole_letter_rank_{alpha_name}\', unsc, perm)\n        inv = invert_perm(perm)\n        sweep(f\'hole_letter_rank_{alpha_name}_inv\', apply_perm(CT, inv), inv)\n\n# indices (0-27)\nhole_cols_seq = [c for (r, c) in HOLES]  # 107 col indices (0-32)\n\nfor name, seq in [(\'row_seq\', hole_rows_seq), (\'col_seq\', hole_cols_seq)]:\n    # Use as rank key for K4\n    seq97 = [v % 97 for v in seq[:97]]\n    perm = rank_perm(seq97)\n    unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n    sweep(f\'hole_{name}_rank97\', unsc, perm)\n\n    # As columnar transposition key\n    for W in [26, 28]:\n        key_vals = seq[:W]\n        col_order = sorted(range(W), key=lambda i: key_vals[i])\n        n_rows = math.ceil(97 / W)\n        perm2 = []\n        for c in col_order:\n            for rr in range(n_rows):\n                idx = rr * W + c\n                if idx < 97: perm2.append(idx)\n        if len(perm2) == 97 and len(set(perm2)) == 97:\n            unsc2 = apply_perm(CT, perm2)\n            sweep(f\'hole_{name}_columnar_W{W}\', unsc2, perm2)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH F: Extract letter positions → ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH F: Hole-sequence as K4 reading order (various mappings) ──")\n\n# F1: Hole sequence rank = K4 reading order\n# Read K4 in the order: first the chars that rank lowest in the hole-letter sequence\nfor alpha_name, alpha in [(\'KA\', KA), (\'AZ\', AZ)]:\n    if len(all_hole_letters_str) < 97: enumerate(e97)]\n    perm = [i for (_, i) in sorted(vals)]\n    unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n    sweep(f\'hole_letter_stable_rank_{alpha_name}\', unsc, perm)\n\n# F2: Grille defines a one-time-pad: XOR K4 with extract (modular)\n# Treat each K4 char and extract char as numbers in KA/AZ, XOR them\nfor alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n    n = len(alpha)\n    result = []\n    for i in range(97):\n        c_ct = alpha.index(CT[i]) if CT[i] in alpha else 0\n        c_ex = alpha.index(GRILLE_EXTRACT[i]) if GRILLE_EXTRACT[i] in alpha else 0\n        result.append(alpha[(c_ct ^ c_ex) % n])\n    pt_xor = \'\'.join(result)\n    has1 = CRIB1 in pt_xor; has2 = CRIB2 in pt_xor\n    sc = quad_score(pt_xor)\n    if has1 or has2:\n        print(f"  *** XOR CRIB HIT *** {alpha_name}: {pt_xor}")\n    top_scores.append((sc, f\'xor_{alpha_name}\', CT, \'XOR\', \'xor\', alpha_name, pt_xor))\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH G: Hole positions define GROUPS — each group = one "strip"\n# K4 carved as strips, strips reordered by grille row order\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH G: Hole-row groupings as strip reordering ──")\n# For each row of the mask with holes, those holes correspond to K4 strip positions\n# The order in which rows appear with holes is the intended reading order of strips\n\n# Find which rows have holes and how many\nhole_row_counts = defaultdict(int)\nfor r, c in HOLES:\n    hole_row_counts[r] += 1\n\n# If K4 is divided into |mask_rows_with_holes| strips...\n# For each divisor d of 97, split K4 into strips of size d or near-d\n# and reorder strips by the grille\'s row order\nrows_with_holes = sorted(hole_row_counts.keys())\nn_strips = len(rows_with_holes)\nprint(f"  Rows with holes: {n_strips} rows ({rows_with_holes})")\n\n# For strip sizes that divide near 97\nfor strip_size in range(1, 20):\n    n_full = 97 // strip_size\n    remainder = 97 % strip_size\n    # Reorder K4 strips based on hole-row order\n    # Only makes sense if n_full ≈ n_strips\n    if abs(n_full - n_strips) <= 3:\n        strips = []\n        for s in range(n_full):\n            strips.append(list(range(s*strip_size, (s+1)*strip_size)))\n        if remainder:\n            strips.append(list(range(n_full*strip_size, 97)))\n        # Reorder by grille: use rows_with_holes as new strip order\n        # rows_with_holes has n_strips elements; use them as permutation of strip indices\n        if len(rows_with_holes) == len(strips):\n            # Map: original_strip[i] goes to position rows_with_holes[i] in output\n            # Or: reorder strips so that strip originally at rows_with_holes[i] comes first\n            col_perm = sorted(range(len(strips)),\n                              key=lambda i: rows_with_holes[i] if i < len(rows_with_holes) else 999)\n            perm = []\n            for si in col_perm:\n                perm.extend(strips[si])\n            if len(perm) == 97 and len(set(perm)) == 97:\n                unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n                sweep(f\'strip_grille_row_s{strip_size}\', unsc, perm)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH H: Backsolve — what permutation makes ABSCISSA/KRYPTOS work?\n# ABSCISSA decrypts ENE crib; KRYPTOS decrypts BC crib.\n# If carved text[21:34] Vigenere(ABSCISSA) = EASTNORTHEAST,\n# and carved text[63:74] Vigenere(KRYPTOS) = BERLINCLOCK,\n# then at carved positions 21-33, ABSCISSA must decrypt the characters.\n# Under the scrambled paradigm: carved[21:34] → after permutation → some position\n# For ABSCISSA-Vig: carved[i] + ABSCISSA[key_pos] = PT[i] (this is already satisfied at pos 21-33)\n# So the question is: what permutation gives us a FULL coherent PT?\n#\n# Insight: try all 97 starting offsets of the ABSCISSA key + look for ENE/BC anywhere\n# This is the "crib drag" approach, but applied AFTER various permutations\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH H: Crib-drag for ABSCISSA/KRYPTOS on K4 variants ──")\n# For each candidate permutation, apply ABSCISSA/KRYPTOS at all offsets\n# (This is computationally feasible since we have ~300 candidates × 97 offsets × 2 keys)\n# But actually we want to find WHICH permutation gives crib at EXPECTED position\n\n# More targeted: if the permutation brings ENE to positions 21-33 in the unscrambled CT,\n# and ABSCISSA is the key, then:\n# real_CT[21:34] Vig_inv(ABSCISSA, real_CT[21:34]) = EASTNORTHEAST\n# This means real_CT[21:34] is determined by ABSCISSA × EASTNORTHEAST\nALPHA = AZ\nkw_abscissa = [ALPHA.index(k) for k in \'ABSCISSA\']\nkw_kryptos  = [ALPHA.index(k) for k in \'KRYPTOS\']\n\nene_ct = \'\'.join(ALPHA[(ALPHA.index(CRIB1[i]) + kw_abscissa[i % len(kw_abscissa)]) % 26]\n                 for i in range(len(CRIB1)))\nbc_ct  = \'\'.join(ALPHA[(ALPHA.index(CRIB2[i]) + kw_kryptos[i % len(kw_kryptos)]) % 26]\n                 for i in range(len(CRIB2)))\nprint(f"  ENE → CT under ABSCISSA-vig: {ene_ct}")\nprint(f"  BC  → CT under KRYPTOS-vig:  {bc_ct}")\n\n# These are what the real CT should contain at the crib positions.\n# Search for these sequences in the K4 carved text to find where they ARE.\nfor target, label in [(ene_ct, \'ENE_ct_ABSCISSA\'), (bc_ct, \'BC_ct_KRYPTOS\')]:\n    pos = CT.find(target)\n    if pos >= 0:\n        print(f"  FOUND {label} at carved position {pos}: {CT[pos:pos+len(target)]}")\n    else:\n        # Check partial matches\n        best_match = max(range(97-len(target)+1), key=lambda i: sum(CT[i+j]==target[j] for j in range(len(target))))\n        n_match = sum(CT[best_match+j]==target[j] for j in range(len(target)))\n        print(f"  {label} not found in K4. Best match {n_match}/{len(target)} at pos {best_match}")\n\n# Also check BEAUFORT and VARIANT-BEAUFORT versions\nfor kw, kw_name in [(\'ABSCISSA\', \'ABSCISSA\'), (\'KRYPTOS\', \'KRYPTOS\')]:\n    for fname, fn in [(\'vig\', vig), (\'beau\', beau), (\'vbeau\', vbeau)]:\n        for aname, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n            # Encrypt each crib\n            crib_target = CRIB1 if kw_name == \'ABSCISSA\' else CRIB2\n            # "encrypt" crib to get expected CT sequence\n            kv = [alpha.index(k) for k in kw if k in alpha]\n            if not kv: continue\n            if fname == \'vig\':\n                ct_seq = \'\'.join(alpha[(alpha.index(c)+kv[i%len(kv)])%len(alpha)]\n                                 for i,c in enumerate(crib_target) if c in alpha)\n            elif fname == \'beau\':\n                ct_seq = \'\'.join(alpha[(kv[i%len(kv)]+alpha.index(c))%len(alpha)]\n                                 for i,c in enumerate(crib_target) if c in alpha)\n            else:  # vbeau\n                ct_seq = \'\'.join(alpha[(alpha.index(c)-kv[i%len(kv)])%len(alpha)]\n                                 for i,c in enumerate(crib_target) if c in alpha)\n            pos = CT.find(ct_seq)\n            if pos >= 0:\n                print(f"  FOUND expected CT for {kw_name}/{fname}/{aname} at carved pos {pos}: {ct_seq}")\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH I: Direct search — for each known keyword, apply at all positions\n# and check if ANY permutation can place both cribs\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH I: Positional crib constraint solver ──")\n# The key insight: under the SCRAMBLED model, the real CT contains the crib sequences.\n# We know real CT is a permutation of carved K4.\n# So: does K4 contain the characters of EASTNORTHEAST in some order?\n# If we can find which 13 positions of K4 contain those characters (with the right key),\n# those positions tell us where ENE is in the real CT.\n\n# For ABSCISSA / Vigenère:\nkw = \'ABSCISSA\'; alpha = AZ\n# ENE in PT means: real_CT[pos+i] = EASTNORTHEAST[i] ENCRYPTED by ABSCISSA[i%8]\n# For a CYCLIC key ABSCISSA (len=8), the expected CT chars at position P are:\n# CT[P+i] = (PT[i] + ABSCISSA[i%8]) mod 26\n\n# Let\'s try: for each starting position P (0-96), compute expected real CT chars\n# Then check if those chars (in that order) appear as a subsequence of K4 carved text\n# This is harder than it sounds — we need them as consecutive positions in the real CT\n\n# Simpler: check if the 13-char expected-CT for ENE is a SUBSTRING of K4\n# (which means K4 contains ENE real-CT at some consecutive positions)\nkv_ab = [AZ.index(k) for k in \'ABSCISSA\']\nfor P in range(85):  # ENE length 13, so P can be 0..84\n    expected_ct = \'\'.join(AZ[(AZ.index(CRIB1[i]) + kv_ab[i%8]) % 26]\n                          for i in range(13))\n    if expected_ct in CT:\n        print(f"  ABSCISSA/vig: ENE expected CT \'{expected_ct}\' found at carved pos {CT.find(expected_ct)} (key offset P={P})")\n    # Try Beaufort\n    expected_ct_b = \'\'.join(AZ[(kv_ab[i%8] - AZ.index(CRIB1[i])) % 26]\n                             for i in range(13))\n    if expected_ct_b in CT:\n        print(f"  ABSCISSA/beau: ENE expected CT \'{expected_ct_b}\' found at carved pos {CT.find(expected_ct_b)} (key offset P={P})")\n\n# The key offset P matters when the key is not fixed-start\n# For key offset P: ABSCISSA[P%8, (P+1)%8, ...]\nfor P in range(8):\n    kv_ab_offset = [kv_ab[(P+i)%8] for i in range(13)]\n    expected_ct = \'\'.join(AZ[(AZ.index(CRIB1[i]) + kv_ab_offset[i]) % 26]\n                          for i in range(13))\n    if expected_ct in CT:\n        print(f"  ABSCISSA/vig offset {P}: ENE expected CT \'{expected_ct}\' FOUND AT CARVED POS {CT.find(expected_ct)}")\n    expected_ct_b = \'\'.join(AZ[(kv_ab_offset[i] - AZ.index(CRIB1[i])) % 26]\n                             for i in range(13))\n    if expected_ct_b in CT:\n        print(f"  ABSCISSA/beau offset {P}: BC expected CT \'{expected_ct_b}\' FOUND AT CARVED POS {CT.find(expected_ct_b)}")\n\nkv_kr = [AZ.index(k) for k in \'KRYPTOS\']\nfor P in range(7):\n    kv_kr_offset = [kv_kr[(P+i)%7] for i in range(11)]\n    expected_ct = \'\'.join(AZ[(AZ.index(CRIB2[i]) + kv_kr_offset[i]) % 26]\n                          for i in range(11))\n    if expected_ct in CT:\n        print(f"  KRYPTOS/vig offset {P}: BC expected CT \'{expected_ct}\' FOUND AT CARVED POS {CT.find(expected_ct)}")\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH J: Structured permutations using hole geometry\n# The 107 holes have a 2D structure. Their (row,col) pairs form a "point cloud".\n# Use PCA-like ordering (project onto major axis) as permutation key.\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH J: Hole geometry — projected orderings ──")\n# Project holes onto various directions\ndef project_and_rank(holes, angle_deg):\n    """Rank holes by projection onto direction given by angle (degrees)."""\n    import math\n    th = math.radians(angle_deg)\n    cos_t, sin_t = math.cos(th), math.sin(th)\n    projs = [r * sin_t + c * cos_t for (r, c) in holes]\n    return sorted(range(len(holes)), key=lambda i: projs[i])\n\nfor angle in [0, 30, 45, 60, 90, 120, 135, 150]:\n    order = project_and_rank(HOLES, angle)\n    # order[i] = which hole comes i-th when projected\n    # Map the first 97 unique K4 positions\n    vals = [(HOLES[i][0] * 33 + HOLES[i][1]) % 97 for i in order]\n    seen = set(); perm = []\n    for v in vals:\n        if v not in seen: seen.add(v); perm.append(v)\n        if len(perm) == 97: break\n    if len(perm) == 97:\n        unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n        sweep(f\'hole_proj_angle{angle}\', unsc, perm)\n\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# APPROACH K: The 106-char extract itself is the PT (not the extract (trimmed to 97)\n# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n── APPROACH K: Extract as PT — solve for key ──")\n# If PT = GRILLE_EXTRACT[:97] and CT = K4 carved, what key does Vigenère need?\n# Vig: CT[i] = (PT[i] + key[i%len]) mod 26\n# key[i] = (CT[i] - PT[i]) mod 26\n# This gives a running key; check if it has a pattern\nfor alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n    n = len(alpha)\n    pt97 = GRILLE_EXTRACT[:97]\n    derived_key = []\n    for i in range(97):\n        if CT[i] in alpha and pt97[i] in alpha:\n            k = (alpha.index(CT[i]) - alpha.index(pt97[i])) % n\n            derived_key.append(alpha[k])\n        else:\n            derived_key.append(\'?\')\n    key_str = \'\'.join(derived_key)\n    print(f" derived_key[i+period])\n        if matches > 85:\n            print(f"    Period {period}: {matches}/97 matches — strong periodicity!")\n\n# key=lambda i: alpha.index(kw[i]) if kw[i] in alpha else 99)\n        n_rows = math.ceil(97 / W)\n        perm = []\n        for c in col_order:\n            for r in range(n_rows):\n                idx = r * W + c\n                if idx < 97: perm.append(idx)\n        if len(perm) == 97 and len(set(perm)) == 97:\n            unsc = apply_perm(CT, perm)\n            sweep(f\'kw_columnar_{kw}_{alpha_name}\', unsc, perm)\n            inv = invert_perm(perm)\n            sweep(f\'kw_columnar_{kw}_{alpha_name}_inv\', apply_perm(CT, inv), inv)\n\n# The key constraint is that with KRYPTOS\'s period of 7, ENE must start at a position p where p ≡ 0 (mod 7), and BC must start at position q where q ≡ 2 (mod 7), with their ranges not overlapping in the 97-character ciphertext. None of the standard approaches with the normal alphabet are matching, so I\'m trying the KRYPTOS alphabet variant where the key letters map to different positions.\n\nComputing the actual key that would decrypt the ciphertext to BERLINCLOCK position by position, I\'m getting M, U, Y, K, L, G... which doesn\'t align with KRYPTOS at all, so either the key alignment is different or I\'m missing something about how the cipher was applied.\n\nBut then I notice something: at position 65, the ciphertext P should decrypt to R (from the Bean-EQ constraint), and when I check, KRYPTOS[65%7] = KRYPTOS[2] = Y does produce that result correctly. So KRYPTOS works for position 65 but apparently not for position 63, which is confusing.\n\nI think I need to reconsider what the actual constraints are—maybe the positions 63-73 for BERLINCLOCK and 21-33 for EASTNORTHEAST aren\'t exact, or maybe K4 uses a different key than KRYPTOS, or the plaintext positions are being interpreted differently under the scrambling model.\n\nLet me think about this more carefully: if the real ciphertext is a permutation of the carved K4, and we decrypt it with a periodic key, then for the cribs to work, the real ciphertext values at positions 21-33 and 63-73 must satisfy specific relationships with the key. For a period-7 key, the key values repeat every 7 positions, so positions 21 and 28 both use key[0], which means the real ciphertext characters at those positions must differ by exactly the difference between the plaintext letters at those positions (E and T, a difference of 15). This gives me a constraint: I need to find pairs of characters in K4 that differ by 15, then check if placing them at the right positions in the real ciphertext allows the rest of the cribs to work out consistently. But the real constraint is positional—I need specific positions in K4 to contain those characters, not just that the characters exist somewhere in the set.\n\nNow I\'m shifting to a strip cipher brute force approach. For manageable computation, I\'m focusing on strip widths 11-14: width 14 gives 7 strips with 5,040 possible orderings, width 13 gives 8 strips with 40,320 orderings, and widths 11-12 each give 9 strips with around 362,880 orderings. I can also use the known crib positions to constrain the search—if ENE is at plaintext position 21 with period-7 Vigenère, that fixes the key values at positions 21-33, and BC at position 63 fixes positions 63-69, which together determine the full 7-character key.\n\nFrom the Bean equation at position 27, if the plaintext is R and the carved ciphertext is P, the key at that position modulo 7 must be Y. But KRYPTOS has S at position 6, not Y, so if the key is KRYPTOS, then position 27 must map to a real ciphertext position where the period-7 index equals 2 (where Y appears in KRYPTOS), giving me a constraint on the permutation σ.\n\nBoth carved positions 27 and 65 contain P and both decrypt to R, so both must map to real ciphertext positions congruent to 2 modulo 7—there are only 14 such positions available, which significantly constrains which permutation σ could work.\n\nNow I\'m computing what the actual ciphertext should be at positions 21-33 when the plaintext EASTONEASTONE is encrypted with the KRYPTOS key, working through each position\'s addition modulo 26 to get the sequence ORQIGCJDYCPLH.\n\nFor the Bean-EQ constraint, I need to figure out where position 27 in the real ciphertext (which is J) maps back to in the carved K4 text. Since the real ciphertext at position 27 equals the carved text at some permuted position, and J appears in K4 at positions 40, 51, or 81, one of those must be the pre-image. I can verify this is consistent by checking that the plaintext at position 27 works out to R under the KRYPTOS key, which matches what the Bean-EQ requires.\n\nFor position 65, the real ciphertext should be P, and interestingly K4 has P at position 65 itself—suggesting the permutation might preserve this position. Now I\'m computing the full real ciphertext across all 97 positions by applying the KRYPTOS-Vigenère cipher to the known plaintext segments, starting with the BERLINCLOCK portion.\n\nWorking through positions 63-73 with the key cycling through KRYPTOS, I get LVPABBUVFAZ, but this creates a problem: the multiset requires 2 V\'s while K4 only contains 1 V, which breaks the constraint again.\n\nThis is actually revealing—it means BC at positions 63-73 under KRYPTOS-vig is mathematically impossible with K4\'s character inventory. Let me check if BC works at different positions by testing each offset modulo 7, since that determines which key character starts the encryption. Most offsets fail due to repeated letters K4 doesn\'t have enough of, but a few like offset 2 and 4 have all unique characters that K4 can provide.\n\nThis suggests BC might actually be at position 65 instead of 63, which would shift the key offset to 2 and make it feasible. So either the claimed position is off by a couple characters, or the actual plaintext location is different from what\'s been assumed. Let me test what happens if I combine ENE at position 21 with BC at position 65 instead—that would use two different key offsets and require checking if K4 has enough of each letter across both segments.\n\nLooking at the character requirements for both segments together, K4 appears to have sufficient copies of every needed letter, so this configuration is actually feasible. Now I need to figure out which specific positions in K4 map to these real ciphertext positions through the permutation, since each real CT position p requires K4 at the inverse permutation position to contain the expected plaintext character.\n\nRather than manually tracking all these positions, I should write a script that systematically tests this: for each possible pair of positions where ENE and BC could appear, I\'ll compute what the real ciphertext must be at those locations, verify the multiset constraint, then attempt to find a valid permutation that maps K4\'s characters to those positions while producing coherent English plaintext for the remaining 73 positions. I\'m indexing through K4 to find where each expected character appears, and I\'m noticing a critical constraint: Y only appears once at position 64, but I need it at position 75 for the BC ciphertext. For BC, I need to find which position encrypts to Y and what offset pattern that requires.\n\nLooking at BC\'s characters, position 10 is K, which gives Y when the key character is O—that\'s KRYPTOS[5], so the offset must satisfy q ≡ 2 mod 7. Position 8 is O, which also produces Y but requires KRYPTOS[0]=K, giving q ≡ 6 mod 7. The other L characters in BC can\'t produce Y since they\'d need N, which isn\'t in the key. So Y appears in BC\'s expected ciphertext at positions where q ≡ 2 mod 7.\n\nFor BC to avoid Y entirely, q can\'t be congruent to 2 or 6 mod 7, leaving offsets 0, 1, 3, 4, 5 as possibilities. Testing offset 4 fails immediately because Y appears in the BC characters but shouldn\'t. Offset 5 also fails—combining ENE with BC requires 4 C\'s total, but K4 only has 2 C\'s available. Offset 6 is next to check.\n\nNow I\'m testing offset 6 with the sequence XGQDYTI, which contains Y at position 8—but ENE@21 already uses the single Y in K4, so this creates a conflict. That rules out offset 6 as well.\n\nSo under the KRYPTOS-vig model with ENE at positions 21-33, only offset 3 works for BC. But the historical analysis places BC at positions 63-73, which corresponds to offset 0, not offset 3. Let me verify offset 0 at position 63: the characters are LVPABBUVFAZ with V appearing twice. K4 has exactly 2 V\'s and ENE uses none, so the V count works out. Checking the full multiset for ENE@21 plus BC@63, every letter count matches what\'s available in K4—this is actually feasible. I\'m verifying that K4 contains all the character frequencies needed for both crib positions—and it does. Every required character appears in sufficient quantity, which means there\'s actually a valid permutation of K4 that could place the traditional cribs at positions 21 and 63 under this scrambled KRYPTOS-vig model.\n\nNow I need to figure out which specific K4 positions map to which ciphertext positions. The Y character is the critical constraint here—it only appears once in K4 at position 64, and the ENE crib requires Y at position 29, so that mapping is forced. For the BC crib, the two V\'s in K4 (at positions 24 and 66) must map to the two V positions in the ciphertext (64 and 70), which gives me only two possible arrangements. These tight constraints significantly reduce the search space, so I\'ll write code to systematically search for permutations that satisfy all the crib mappings at once. Writing constraint solver... Characters like M that don\'t appear in the crib requirements can map to any of the remaining 73 positions, giving me flexibility. The core insight is that once I fix the full permutation, the quadgram score is completely determined—so I need to evaluate the complete decryption for each valid assignment rather than trying to optimize the free positions independently.\n\nThe real challenge is that 73 of the 97 plaintext positions are unconstrained by the cribs, and for a random permutation of those positions, the output would be gibberish. I need to find the specific permutation that makes all 97 positions read as coherent English, which suggests either hill-climbing on the free positions or exploiting the strip cipher structure to constrain the search space further.\n\nIf this is a strip transposition with width 12, the crib positions at real CT indices 21-33 and 63-73 would map to specific strips, which severely limits which K4 positions can contain the crib characters and how those strips can be arranged.\n\nRather than continue this theoretical analysis, I\'m going to implement a brute-force strip attack directly. I\'ll iterate through strip widths 8-16, enumerate permutations for each width, apply the KRYPTOS-vig cipher, and check for crib matches. With 12 strips giving roughly 362K orderings and KRYPTOS-vig as the primary cipher to test, this should run efficiently.', signature='')]

So carved pos 64 MUST go to real CT pos 29.\n  This is a FORCED constraint, regardless of which permutation we use.\n\n  The 24 crib positions give 24 equations: real_CT[crib_pos] = specific K4 char.\n  → Each such char must come from a specific set of K4 positions.\n  → This severely constrains the permutation.\n\nApproach:\n  1. Crib constraint analysis: list ALL valid K4-position assignments for each crib position\n  2. Strip cipher attack: enumerate all strip orderings (W=8..16), check for cribs\n  3. Rotation attack: all 97 rotations of K4, apply all keys\n  4. Guided permutation: fix forced assignments, optimize rest with hill-climbing\n"""\n\nimport sys, json, math, os, itertools\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nsys.path.insert(0, \'src\')\nfrom kryptos.kernel.constants import CT\n\nK4 = CT  # 97 chars carved K4\nassert len(K4) == 97\n\nGRILLE_EXTRACT = \'HJLVACINXZHUYOCMWSEAFYBZACJFHIFXRYVFIJMXEILLNELJNXZKILKRDINPADMNVZACEIMUWAFGIMUKRGILVHNQXWYABXZKIKJUFQRXCD\'\nKA  = \'KRYPTOSABCDEFGHIJLMNQUVWXZ\'\nAZ  = \'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ\'\nCRIB1 = \'EASTNORTHEAST\'\nCRIB2 = \'BERLINCLOCK\'\nKEYWORDS = [\'KRYPTOS\',\'PALIMPSEST\',\'ABSCISSA\',\'SHADOW\',\'BERLIN\',\'CLOCK\',\n            \'NORTH\',\'EAST\',\'IQLUSION\',\'ILLUSION\',\'EQUINOX\']\n\n# ── Quadgrams ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n_QUAD = json.load(open(\'data/english_quadgrams.json\'))\n_FLOOR = min(_QUAD.values()) - 1.0\ndef quad_score(text):\n    if len(text) < 4: return _FLOOR\n    s = sum(_QUAD.get(text[i:i+4], _FLOOR) for i in range(len(text)-3))\n    return s / max(len(text)-3, 1)\n\n# ── Cipher engines ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef vig_dec(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = [alpha.index(k) for k in key if k in alpha]; n=len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out=[]; ki=0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha: out.append(alpha[(alpha.index(c)-kv[ki%len(kv)])%n]); ki+=1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef beau_dec(ct, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = [alpha.index(k) for k in key if k in alpha]; n=len(alpha)\n    if not kv: return \'\'\n    out=[]; ki=0\n    for c in ct:\n        if c in alpha: out.append(alpha[(kv[ki%len(kv)]-alpha.index(c))%n]); ki+=1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef vig_enc(pt, key, alpha=AZ):\n    kv = [alpha.index(k) for k in key if k in alpha]; n=len(alpha)\n    out=[]; ki=0\n    for c in pt:\n        if c in alpha: out.append(alpha[(alpha.index(c)+kv[ki%len(kv)])%n]); ki+=1\n        else: out.append(c)\n    return \'\'.join(out)\n\ndef apply_perm(seq, perm):\n    return \'\'.join(seq[perm[i]] for i in range(len(perm)))\n\n# ── K4 character positions lookup ──────────────────────────────────────────\nchar_positions = defaultdict(list)\nfor i, c in enumerate(K4):\n    char_positions[c].append(i)\n\nprint("K4 character counts:")\nfor c in sorted(char_positions.keys()):\n    print(f"  {c}: {len(char_positions[c])} at {char_positions[c]}")\n\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# PART 1: CRIB CONSTRAINT ANALYSIS\n# For KRYPTOS-Vigenère, compute expected real CT chars at crib positions\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n" + "="*70)\nprint("PART 1: CRIB CONSTRAINT ANALYSIS (KRYPTOS-vig)")\nprint("="*70)\n\nKRYPTOS = \'KRYPTOS\'\nKRYPTOS_vals = [AZ.index(k) for k in KRYPTOS]\n\ndef compute_expected_ct(plaintext, key_vals, start_pos):\n    """Compute expected real CT chars if PT starts at real CT position start_pos."""\n    result = []\n    for i, pt_char in enumerate(plaintext):\n        pt_val = AZ.index(pt_char)\n        key_val = key_vals[(start_pos + i) % len(key_vals)]\n        result.append(AZ[(pt_val + key_val) % 26])\n    return \'\'.join(result)\n\n# For each possible ENE start position (0..84) and BC start position (0..86)\n# compute expected real CT and check multiset feasibility\n\nbest_combos = []\n\nfor ene_start in range(85):\n    ene_ct = compute_expected_ct(CRIB1, KRYPTOS_vals, ene_start)\n    ene_multiset = defaultdict(int)\n    for c in ene_ct: ene_multiset[c] += 1\n    # Check ENE feasibility\n    ene_ok = all(ene_multiset[c] <= len(char_positions[c]) for c in ene_multiset)\n    if not ene_ok: continue\n\n    for bc_start in range(87):\n        # No overlap\n        if ene_start <= bc_start <= ene_start + 12: continue\n        if bc_start <= ene_start <= bc_start + 10: continue\n\n        bc_ct = compute_expected_ct(CRIB2, KRYPTOS_vals, bc_start)\n        # Combined multiset\n        combined = defaultdict(int)\n        for c in ene_ct: combined[c] += 1\n        for c in bc_ct: combined[c] += 1\n        # Check feasibility\n        ok = all(combined[c] <= len(char_positions[c]) for c in combined)\n        if ok:\n            # Score: prefer positions matching known hints (21, 63)\n            ene_score = abs(ene_start - 21)\n            bc_score = abs(bc_start - 63)\n            best_combos.append((ene_score + bc_score, ene_start, bc_start, ene_ct, bc_ct))\n\nbest_combos.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])\nprint(f"Feasible (ENE_start, BC_start) pairs under KRYPTOS-vig: {len(best_combos)}")\nprint("Top 10 closest to (21, 63):")\nfor score, es, bs, ect, bct in best_combos[:10]:\n    print(f"  ENE@{es:2d} BC@{bs:2d}  ENE_ct={ect}  BC_ct={bct}")\n\n# Also try KRYPTOS-Beaufort\nprint("\\n\\nKRYPTOS-Beaufort feasibility:")\nbest_combos_beau = []\nfor ene_start in range(85):\n    ene_ct_b = \'\'.join(AZ[(KRYPTOS_vals[(ene_start+i)%7] - AZ.index(CRIB1[i])) % 26]\n                        for i in range(len(CRIB1)))\n    ec_ms = defaultdict(int)\n    for c in ene_ct_b: ec_ms[c] += 1\n    if not all(ec_ms[c] <= len(char_positions[c]) for c in ec_ms): continue\n    for bc_start in range(87):\n        if ene_start <= bc_start <= ene_start+12: continue\n        if bc_start <= ene_start <= bc_start+10: continue\n        bc_ct_b = \'\'.join(AZ[(KRYPTOS_vals[(bc_start+i)%7] - AZ.index(CRIB2[i])) % 26]\n                           for i in range(len(CRIB2)))\n        comb = defaultdict(int)\n        for c in ene_ct_b+bc_ct_b: comb[c] += 1\n        if all(comb[c] <= len(char_positions[c]) for c in comb):\n            best_combos_beau.append((abs(ene_start-21)+abs(bc_start-63), ene_start, bc_start, ene_ct_b, bc_ct_b))\n\nbest_combos_beau.sort()\nprint(f"Feasible pairs (Beaufort): {len(best_combos_beau)}")\nfor score, es, bs, ect, bct in best_combos_beau[:5]:\n    print(f"  ENE@{es:2d} BC@{bs:2d}  ENE_ct={ect}  BC_ct={bct}")\n\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# PART 2: FORCED CONSTRAINTS\n# Find characters with count=1 in K4 → their real CT position is FORCED\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n" + "="*70)\nprint("PART 2: FORCED CONSTRAINTS (unique characters)")\nprint("="*70)\n\n# Characters appearing exactly once in K4\nunique_chars = {c: pos[0] for c, pos in char_positions.items() if len(pos) == 1}\nprint(f"Unique characters in K4: {unique_chars}")\n\n# For the top feasible combo (ENE@21, BC@63 under KRYPTOS-vig):\nif best_combos:\n    _, ene_s, bc_s, ene_expected, bc_expected = best_combos[0]\n    print(f"\\nBest combo: ENE@{ene_s} BC@{bc_s}")\n    print(f"ENE expected CT: {ene_expected}")\n    print(f"BC expected CT:  {bc_expected}")\n\n    forced = {}  # real_CT_position → carved_K4_position (FORCED, unique char)\n    print("\\nForced assignments (unique chars in expected CT):")\n    for i, c in enumerate(ene_expected):\n        if c in unique_chars:\n            real_pos = ene_s + i\n            k4_pos = unique_chars[c]\n            print(f"  real_CT[{real_pos}] = {c} → carved K4 position {k4_pos} (FORCED)")\n            forced[real_pos] = k4_pos\n    for i, c in enumerate(bc_expected):\n        if c in unique_chars:\n            real_pos = bc_s + i\n            k4_pos = unique_chars[c]\n            print(f"  real_CT[{real_pos}] = {c} → carved K4 position {k4_pos} (FORCED)")\n            forced[real_pos] = k4_pos\n\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# PART 3: ROTATION ATTACK\n# Try all 97 rotations of K4, apply all keywords/ciphers\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n" + "="*70)\nprint("PART 3: Rotation attack (97 rotations × keywords)")\nprint("="*70)\n\nrotation_hits = []\nrotation_scores = []\nfor offset in range(97):\n    rotated = K4[offset:] + K4[:offset]\n    for kw in KEYWORDS:\n        for alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ), (\'KA\', KA)]:\n            for fname, fn in [(\'vig\', vig_dec), (\'beau\', beau_dec)]:\n                pt = fn(rotated, kw, alpha)\n                has1 = CRIB1 in pt; has2 = CRIB2 in pt\n                if has1 or has2:\n                    print(f"  CRIB HIT: rotation {offset}, {kw}/{fname}/{alpha_name}")\n                    print(f"  PT: {pt}")\n                    rotation_hits.append({\'offset\': offset, \'kw\': kw, \'cipher\': fname,\n                                         \'alpha\': alpha_name, \'pt\': pt,\n                                         \'has1\': has1, \'has2\': has2})\n                sc = quad_score(pt)\n                rotation_scores.append((sc, offset, kw, fname, alpha_name, pt))\n\nrotation_scores.sort(reverse=True)\nprint(f"Total rotation candidates: {len(rotation_scores)}")\nprint(f"CRIB HITS: {len(rotation_hits)}")\nprint("Top 5 rotation scores:")\nfor sc, off, kw, fn, an, pt in rotation_scores[:5]:\n    print(f"  offset={off:2d} {kw}/{fn}/{an}: {sc:.4f} | {pt[:40]}...")\n\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# PART 4: STRIP CIPHER BRUTE FORCE\n# For widths where strip_count is small (factorial is manageable)\n# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nprint("\\n" + "="*70)\nprint("PART 4: Strip cipher brute force (W=13,14,15,16)")\nprint("="*70)\n\nstrip_hits = []\nstrip_best = []\n\ndef build_strips(text, W):\n    """Split text into strips of width W."""\n    strips = []\n    i = 0\n    while i < len(text):\n        strips.append(text[i:i+W])\n        i += W\n    return strips\n\nfor W in [16, 15, 14, 13, 12]:\n    strips = build_strips(K4, W)\n    n = len(strips)\n    total = math.factorial(n)\n    print(f"  W={W}: {n} strips, {total} orderings")\n    if total > 5_000_000:\n        print(f"    Skipping (too many)")\n        continue\n\n    best_sc = -999.0\n    best_perm = None\n    best_pt = None\n    best_info = None\n    tested = 0\n\n    for perm in itertools.permutations(range(n)):\n        candidate = \'\'.join(strips[perm[i]] for i in range(n))\n        # Fast crib check first (no decryption needed for raw check)\n        for kw in [\'KRYPTOS\', \'ABSCISSA\', \'PALIMPSEST\']:\n            for alpha_name, alpha in [(\'AZ\', AZ)]:\n                for fname, fn in [(\'vig\', vig_dec), (\'beau\', beau_dec)]:\n                    pt = fn(candidate, kw, alpha)\n                    if CRIB1 in pt or CRIB2 in pt:\n    ene_ct = compute_expected_ct(CRIB1, KRYPTOS_vals, ene_start)\n   112→    ene_multiset = defaultdict(int)\n   113→    for c in ene_ct: ene_multiset[c] += 1\n   114→    # Check ENE feasibility\n   115→    ene_ok = all(ene_multiset[c] <= len(char_positions[c]) for c in ene_multiset)\n   116→    if not ene_ok: continue\n   117→\n   118→    for bc_start in range(87):\n   119→        # No overlap\n   120→        if ene_start <= bc_start <= ene_start + 12: continue\n   121→        if bc_start <= ene_start <= bc_start + 10: continue\n   122→\n   123→        bc_ct = compute_expected_ct(CRIB2, KRYPTOS_vals, bc_start)\n   124→        # Combined multiset\n   125→        combined = defaultdict(int)\n   126→        for c in ene_ct: combined[c] += 1\n   127→        for c in bc_ct: combined[c] += 1\n   128→        # Check feasibility\n   129→        ok = all(combined[c] <= len(char_positions[c]) for c in combined)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it [ToolUseBlock(id='toolu_015FQJsSxihFjzRzT1oqdzgq', name='Write', input={'file_path': '/home/cpatrick/kryptos/scripts/e_unscramble_04_crib_pinning.py', 'content': '#!/usr/bin/env python3\n"""\nE-UNSCRAMBLE-04: Crib-Pinning Simulated Annealing\n===================================================\nParadigm: carved_K4 = SCRAMBLE(real_CT)\n          perm[i] = j  →  real_CT[i] = K4[j]\n          Then: PT = decrypt(real_CT, keyword)\n\nKEY INSIGHT from E-UNSCRAMBLE-03:\n  - Constraint-driven search → cribs present, surrounding text random/incoherent\n  - Hill climbing → coherent English, no cribs\n  THIS SCRIPT: pin 24 crib positions (so cribs are guaranteed), then SA-optimize\n  the remaining 73 free positions to maximize quadgram score of full 97-char PT.\n\nExpected behavior:\n  - If correct cipher/keyword/positions: SA converges to coherent English WITH cribs\n  - Score threshold: -4.5+ indicates English-quality text\n  - The FORCED constraint (K4[64]=Y → real_CT[29] for KRYPTOS-vig+ENE@21) narrows search\n\nStrategy:\n  Phase 1: Quick scan (800 SA iters) over ALL feasible (ene_s, bc_s) pairs per config\n  Phase 2: Deep dive (50000 SA iters × 15 seeds) on top-20 pairs + ENE@21/BC@63\n"""\n\nimport sys, json, math, os, time, random\nfrom collections import Counter, defaultdict\n\nsys.path.insert(0, \'src\')\nfrom kryptos.kernel.constants import CT as K4_CT\n\nK4 = K4_CT\nN  = 97\nassert len(K4) == N\n\nAZ  = \'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ\'\nKA  = \'KRYPTOSABCDEFGHIJLMNQUVWXZ\'\n\nCRIB_ENE = \'EASTNORTHEAST\'   # length 13\nCRIB_BC  = \'BERLINCLOCK\'     # length 11\nL_ENE, L_BC = len(CRIB_ENE), len(CRIB_BC)  # 13, 11\n\n# ── Quadgrams ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nprint("[E-04] Loading quadgrams...", flush=True)\n_QG = json.load(open(\'data/english_quadgrams.json\'))\n_MISS = min(_QG.values()) - 2.0   # penalty for unknown quadgrams\n\ndef qscore_str(s):\n    """Average log10-prob quadgram score for string s."""\n    n = len(s)\n    if n < 4: return _MISS\n    return sum(_QG.get(s[i:i+4], _MISS) for i in range(n-3)) / (n-3)\n\nprint(f"  {len(_QG)} quadgrams loaded, MISS={_MISS:.2f}", flush=True)\n\n# ── Lookup tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n_AZI = {c: i for i, c in enumerate(AZ)}   # \'A\'→0 … \'Z\'→25\n\ndef make_kv(keyword, alpha=AZ):\n    """Convert keyword string to list of int key values in given alphabet."""\n    ai = {c: i for i, c in enumerate(alpha)}\n    return [ai[c] for c in keyword if c in ai]\n\n# ── K4 inventory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nK4_COUNTS  = Counter(K4)\nK4_BY_CHAR = defaultdict(list)\nfor _j, _c in enumerate(K4):\n    K4_BY_CHAR[_c].append(_j)\n\nprint("K4 inventory:", dict(sorted(K4_COUNTS.items())), flush=True)\n\n# ── Single-character encrypt/decrypt ──────────────────────────────────────────\ndef pt_char_vig(ct_c, ki):\n    """Vigenère decrypt: PT = (CT - KEY) mod 26"""\n    return AZ[(_AZI[ct_c] - ki) % 26]\n\ndef pt_char_beau(ct_c, ki):\n    """Beaufort decrypt: PT = (KEY - CT) mod 26"""\n    return AZ[(ki - _AZI[ct_c]) % 26]\n\ndef exp_ct_vig(crib, kv, start):\n    """Expected real-CT chars for crib at real-CT position `start` under Vigenère."""\n    kl = len(kv)\n    return [AZ[(_AZI[c] + kv[(start+k)%kl]) % 26] for k, c in enumerate(crib)]\n\ndef exp_ct_beau(crib, kv, start):\n    """Expected real-CT chars for crib at real-CT position `start` under Beaufort."""\n    kl = len(kv)\n    return [AZ[(kv[(start+k)%kl] - _AZI[c]) % 26] for k, c in enumerate(crib)]\n\n# ── Feasibility ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef find_feasible_pairs(kv, is_vig):\n    """Return list of (ene_s, bc_s, ene_exp, bc_exp) that are character-feasible."""\n    exp_fn = exp_ct_vig if is_vig else exp_ct_beau\n    pairs  = []\n    for es in range(N - L_ENE + 1):\n        ee = exp_fn(CRIB_ENE, kv, es)\n        for bs in range(N - L_BC + 1):\n            # Non-overlapping check\n            if not (bs >= es + L_ENE or es >= bs + L_BC):\n                continue\n            be     = exp_fn(CRIB_BC, kv, bs)\n            needed = Counter(ee + be)\n            if all(K4_COUNTS.get(c, 0) >= n for c, n in needed.items()):\n                pairs.append((es, bs, ee, be))\n    return pairs\n\n# ── Build pinned permutation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef build_pinned_perm(es, ee, bs, be, rng):\n    """\n    Build a permutation with the 24 crib positions assigned to character-matching\n    K4 positions. Remaining 73 positions filled randomly.\n    Returns (perm, pinned_set) or (None, None) if infeasible.\n    """\n    perm   = [-1] * N\n    used   = set()\n    pinned = set()\n\n    for start, exp in [(es, ee), (bs, be)]:\n        for k, ch in enumerate(exp):\n            rp     = start + k                          # real-CT position\n            cands  = [j for j in K4_BY_CHAR.get(ch, []) if j not in used]\n            if not cands:\n                return None, None                       # infeasible (shouldn\'t happen)\n            chosen = rng.choice(cands)\n            perm[rp] = chosen\n            used.add(chosen)\n            pinned.add(rp)\n\n    # Fill remaining positions randomly\n    free_r = [i for i in range(N) if perm[i] == -1]\n    free_k = [j for j in range(N) if j not in used]\n    rng.shuffle(free_k)\n    for i, j in zip(free_r, free_k):\n        perm[i] = j\n\n    assert all(x >= 0 for x in perm), "Unfilled perm positions"\n    assert len(set(perm)) == N,        "Duplicate K4 positions in perm"\n    return perm, pinned\n\n# ── Simulated Annealing (incremental quadgram scoring) ────────────────────────\ndef sa_pinned(perm_in, pinned, kv, is_vig, n_iter, rng, T0=10.0, Tf=0.1):\n    """\n    SA with crib positions pinned. Only free (non-pinned) positions are swapped.\n    Uses INCREMENTAL quadgram scoring: only recompute affected quadgrams on each swap.\n\n    Returns (best_perm, best_avg_score, best_pt_str).\n    """\n    kl     = len(kv)\n    free   = [i for i in range(N) if i not in pinned]\n    nf     = len(free)\n    pt_fn  = pt_char_vig if is_vig else pt_char_beau\n\n    p      = perm_in[:]\n\n    # Build PT list (list of single chars for fast mutation)\n    pt = [pt_fn(K4[p[i]], kv[i % kl]) for i in range(N)]\n\n    # Initial total score (sum, not avg — we track sum for incremental updates)\n    total_s = sum(_QG.get(pt[i]+pt[i+1]+pt[i+2]+pt[i+3], _MISS)\n                  for i in range(N-3))\n\n    best_p, best_s, best_pt = p[:], total_s, pt[:]\n    cur_s  = total_s\n\n    # Precompute log-decay rate for temperature schedule\n    log_r = math.log(Tf / T0) if (n_iter > 1 and Tf != T0) else 0.0\n\n    def region_score(lo, hi):\n        return sum(_QG.get(pt[i]+pt[i+1]+pt[i+2]+pt[i+3], _MISS)\n                   for i in range(lo, hi+1))\n\n    for step in range(n_iter):\n        T  = T0 * math.exp(log_r * step / n_iter)\n\n        # Pick two distinct free positions\n        i1 = rng.randint(0, nf-1)\n        i2 = rng.randint(0, nf-2)\n        if i2 >= i1:\n            i2 += 1\n        a, b = free[i1], free[i2]\n\n        # Compute new PT chars after swapping perm[a] ↔ perm[b]\n        new_a = pt_fn(K4[p[b]], kv[a % kl])   # K4[new_perm[a]] with key for pos a\n        new_b = pt_fn(K4[p[a]], kv[b % kl])   # K4[new_perm[b]] with key for pos b\n\n        if new_a == pt[a] and new_b == pt[b]:\n            continue                            # no change, skip\n\n        # Compute affected quadgram ranges\n        # Quadgrams using position x: indices max(0,x-3) to min(N-4,x)\n        lo_a, hi_a = max(0, a-3), min(N-4, a)\n        lo_b, hi_b = max(0, b-3), min(N-4, b)\n\n        # Temporarily update PT chars\n        old_a, old_b = pt[a], pt[b]\n        pt[a] = new_a\n        pt[b] = new_b\n\n        if hi_a < lo_b:\n            # Non-overlapping: compute two separate regions\n            old_contrib = region_score(lo_a, hi_a) + region_score(lo_b, hi_b)\n            # Swap back to get old scores, then update\n            pt[a], pt[b] = old_a, old_b\n            old_c_a = region_score(lo_a, hi_a)\n            old_c_b = region_score(lo_b, hi_b)\n            pt[a], pt[b] = new_a, new_b\n            new_c_a = region_score(lo_a, hi_a)\n            new_c_b = region_score(lo_b, hi_b)\n            delta = (new_c_a - old_c_a) + (new_c_b - old_c_b)\n        else:\n            # Overlapping or adjacent: use combined range\n            lo, hi = min(lo_a, lo_b), max(hi_a, hi_b)\n            pt[a], pt[b] = old_a, old_b\n            old_contrib = region_score(lo, hi)\n            pt[a], pt[b] = new_a, new_b\n            new_contrib  = region_score(lo, hi)\n            delta = new_contrib - old_contrib\n\n        # Accept / reject\n        if delta > 0 or (T > 1e-12 and rng.random() < math.exp(delta / T)):\n            p[a], p[b] = p[b], p[a]\n            cur_s += delta\n            if cur_s > best_s:\n                best_s  = cur_s\n                best_p  = p[:]\n                best_pt = pt[:]\n        else:\n            pt[a] = old_a\n            pt[b] = old_b\n\n    return best_p, best_s / (N-3), \'\'.join(best_pt)\n\n# ── Single-pair attack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef attack_pair(es, ee, bs, be, kv, is_vig, keyword, n_iter, n_seeds, base_seed=0):\n    """Run SA with multiple seeds on a single (es, bs) pair. Return list of result dicts."""\n    cipher  = \'vig\' if is_vig else \'beau\'\n    results = []\n    for seed in range(n_seeds):\n        rng         = random.Random(base_seed + seed * 98765 + es * 103 + bs * 7)\n        perm, pinned = build_pinned_perm(es, ee, bs, be, rng)\n        if perm is None:\n            continue\n        best_p, best_s, best_pt = sa_pinned(perm, pinned, kv, is_vig, n_iter, rng)\n        ene_ok = best_pt[es:es+L_ENE] == CRIB_ENE\n        bc_ok  = best_pt[bs:bs+L_BC]  == CRIB_BC\n        results.append({\n            \'keyword\': keyword, \'cipher\': cipher,\n            \'ene_s\': es, \'bc_s\': bs,\n            \'score\': best_s, \'pt\': best_pt,\n            \'perm\': best_p, \'pinned\': sorted(pinned),\n            \'ene_ok\': ene_ok, \'bc_ok\': bc_ok,\n            \'both\': ene_ok and bc_ok,\n            \'seed\': seed,\n        })\n    return results\n\n# ── Full attack for one (keyword, cipher) config ──────────────────────────────\ndef full_attack(keyword, is_vig,\n                n_p1=800,   n_p1_seeds=1,\n                n_p2=50000, n_p2_seeds=15,\n                n_top=20):\n    """\n    Phase 1: Quick scan over all feasible pairs.\n    Phase 2: Deep SA on top-N pairs + the historically motivated ENE@21/BC@63.\n    """\n    cipher = \'vig\' if is_vig else \'beau\'\n    sep    = \'=\'*70\n    print(f"\\n{sep}", flush=True)\n    print(f"ATTACK: {keyword}/{cipher}", flush=True)\n    print(sep, flush=True)\n\n    kv = make_kv(keyword)\n\n    # ─ Feasible pairs ─\n    t0    = time.time()\n    pairs = find_feasible_pairs(kv, is_vig)\n    print(f"  {len(pairs)} feasible (ene_s, bc_s) pairs [{time.time()-t0:.1f}s]", flush=True)\n    if not pairs:\n        return []\n\n    # ─ Phase 1: Quick scan ─\n    print(f"Phase 1: {n_p1} SA iters × {n_p1_seeds} seed(s) per pair ...", flush=True)\n    t0 = time.time()\n    phase1 = []\n\n    for idx, (es, bs, ee, be) in enumerate(pairs):\n        if idx % 300 == 0 and idx > 0:\n            print(f"  {idx}/{len(pairs)} [{time.time()-t0:.0f}s]", flush=True)\n        best_s = -999.0\n        for sd in range(n_p1_seeds):\n            rng = random.Random(sd * 7777 + es * 97 + bs)\n            pm, pn = build_pinned_perm(es, ee, bs, be, rng)\n            if pm is None:\n                continue\n            _, s, _ = sa_pinned(pm, pn, kv, is_vig, n_p1, rng)\n            best_s   = max(best_s, s)\n        phase1.append((best_s, es, bs, ee, be))\n\n    phase1.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)\n    elapsed = time.time() - t0\n    print(f"  Phase 1 done [{elapsed:.0f}s]. Top-5 pairs:", flush=True)\n    for s, es, bs, _, _ in phase1[:5]:\n        print(f"    ENE@{es:2d} BC@{bs:2d}: {s:.4f}", flush=True)\n\n    # ─ Phase 2: Deep dive ─\n    print(f"\\nPhase 2: {n_p2} SA iters × {n_p2_seeds} seeds on top-{n_top} ...", flush=True)\n    t0 = time.time()\n\n    # Always include the historically motivated ENE@21, BC@63\n    hist   = next((x for x in phase1 if x[1] == 21 and x[2] == 63), None)\n    top    = list(phase1[:n_top])\n    if hist and hist not in top:\n        top.append(hist)\n\n    all_res = []\n\n    for rank, (p1s, es, bs, ee, be) in enumerate(top):\n        # Extra seeds for the historically motivated pair\n        seeds = n_p2_seeds + (10 if es == 21 and bs == 63 else 0)\n        print(f"  [{rank+1:2d}/{len(top)}] ENE@{es:2d} BC@{bs:2d} p1={p1s:.4f} seeds={seeds}",\n              flush=True, end=\'\')\n\n        res = attack_pair(es, ee, bs, be, kv, is_vig, keyword,\n                          n_p2, seeds, base_seed=rank * 100000)\n        if not res:\n            print(" → no valid assignments", flush=True)\n            continue\n\n        res.sort(key=lambda x: x[\'score\'], reverse=True)\n        best = res[0]\n        print(f" → best={best[\'score\']:.4f} both={best[\'both\']}", flush=True)\n\n        if best[\'score\'] > -4.5:\n            print(f"  *** HIGH SCORE *** PT: {best[\'pt\']}", flush=True)\n            print(f"    ENE_ok={best[\'ene_ok\']} BC_ok={best[\'bc_ok\']}", flush=True)\n\n        all_res.extend(res)\n\n    print(f"\\nPhase 2 done [{time.time()-t0:.0f}s]", flush=True)\n\n    # Sort and print top-5\n    all_res.sort(key=lambda x: x[\'score\'], reverse=True)\n    print(f"\\nTop-5 {keyword}/{cipher}:", flush=True)\n    for r in all_res[:5]:\n        print(f"  score={r[\'score\']:.4f} both={r[\'both\']} "\n              f"ENE@{r[\'ene_s\']:2d} BC@{r[\'bc_s\']:2d} seed={r[\'seed\']}", flush=True)\n        print(f"  PT: {r[\'pt\']}", flush=True)\n\n    return all_res\n\n# ── Main ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nprint(f"\\n{\'#\'*70}", flush=True)\nprint("E-UNSCRAMBLE-04: Crib-Pinning Simulated Annealing")\nprint(f"{\'#\'*70}\\n", flush=True)\n\n# Summarise forced constraints for the historically motivated case\nprint("─── Forced constraint analysis: KRYPTOS/vig, ENE@21, BC@63 ───", flush=True)\n_kv_k = make_kv(\'KRYPTOS\')\n_ee21  = exp_ct_vig(CRIB_ENE, _kv_k, 21)\n_be63  = exp_ct_beau(CRIB_BC,  make_kv(\'KRYPTOS\'), 63)  # wrong—use vig\n_be63  = exp_ct_vig(CRIB_BC,  _kv_k, 63)\nprint(f"  ENE expected real-CT: {\'\'.join(_ee21)}", flush=True)\nprint(f"  BC  expected real-CT: {\'\'.join(_be63)}", flush=True)\n_comb  = Counter(_ee21 + _be63)\nprint(f"  Character demands:", flush=True)\nfor c in sorted(_comb):\n    have = K4_COUNTS.get(c, 0)\n    mark = " ← FORCED (only 1 in K4)" if have == _comb[c] else ""\n    print(f"    {c}: need {_comb[c]}, have {have}{mark}", flush=True)\n_feasible = all(K4_COUNTS.get(c,0) >= n for c,n in _comb.items())\nprint(f"  Feasible: {_feasible}\\n", flush=True)\n\n# ── Run configs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nCONFIGS = [\n    (\'KRYPTOS\',    True),    # Vigenère  — most likely\n    (\'KRYPTOS\',    False),   # Beaufort  — strong hill-climb result\n    (\'ABSCISSA\',   True),    # Vigenère\n    (\'ABSCISSA\',   False),   # Beaufort\n    (\'PALIMPSEST\', True),    # Vigenère\n    (\'BERLIN\',     True),    # Vigenère\n    (\'SHADOW\',     True),    # Vigenère\n    (\'CLOCK\',      True),    # Vigenère\n]\n\nall_results = []\n\nfor keyword, is_vig in CONFIGS:\n    try:\n        res = full_attack(\n            keyword, is_vig,\n            n_p1=800,    n_p1_seeds=1,\n            n_p2=50000,  n_p2_seeds=15,\n            n_top=20,\n        )\n        all_results.extend(res)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        import traceback\n        print(f"\\nERROR in {keyword}/{\'vig\' if is_vig else \'beau\'}: {exc}", flush=True)\n        traceback.print_exc()\n\n# ── Global summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nall_results.sort(key=lambda x: x[\'score\'], reverse=True)\n\nprint(f"\\n{\'=\'*70}", flush=True)\nprint("GLOBAL TOP-15 RESULTS", flush=True)\nprint(\'=\'*70, flush=True)\nfor i, r in enumerate(all_results[:15]):\n    print(f"#{i+1:2d}: {r[\'keyword\']}/{r[\'cipher\']} ENE@{r[\'ene_s\']:2d} BC@{r[\'bc_s\']:2d} "\n          f"score={r[\'score\']:.4f} both={r[\'both\']} seed={r[\'seed\']}", flush=True)\n    print(f"      PT: {r[\'pt\']}", flush=True)\n\n# ── Any results with BOTH cribs ───────────────────────────────────────────────\ncrib_hits = [r for r in all_results if r[\'both\']]\nif crib_hits:\n    print(f"\\n{\'=\'*70}", flush=True)\n    print(f"RESULTS WITH BOTH CRIBS CORRECT ({len(crib_hits)} total)", flush=True)\n    print(\'=\'*70, flush=True)\n    for i, r in enumerate(crib_hits[:10]):\n        print(f"#{i+1}: {r[\'keyword\']}/{r[\'cipher\']} ENE@{r[\'ene_s\']} BC@{r[\'bc_s\']} "\n              f"score={r[\'score\']:.4f}", flush=True)\n        print(f"  PT: {r[\'pt\']}", flush=True)\nelse:\n    print("\\n[no results with both cribs confirmed by SA]", flush=True)\n\n# ── Save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nos.makedirs(\'kbot_results\', exist_ok=True)\nsave = {\n    \'experiment\':    \'E-UNSCRAMBLE-04\',\n    \'description\':   \'Crib-pinning SA: 24 crib positions fixed, 73 free positions optimized\',\n    \'timestamp\':     time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\'),\n    \'n_total\':       len(all_results),\n    \'n_crib_hits\':   len(crib_hits),\n    \'global_best\':   {k: v for k, v in all_results[0].items() if k != \'perm\'} if all_results else None,\n    \'crib_hits\':     [{k: v for k, v in r.items() if k != \'perm\'} for r in crib_hits[:50]],\n    \'top_100\':       [{k: v for k, v in r.items() if k != \'perm\'} for r in all_results[:100]],\n}\nwith open(\'kbot_results/unscramble_04.json\', \'w\') as f:\n    json.dump(save, f, indent=2)\nprint(f"\\nSaved {len(all_results)} results to kbot_results/unscramble_04.json", flush=True)\n'})]
